question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026. The official report shows CPI increased only 0.3% in March 2026 on a seasonally adjusted basis, which is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for YES resolution. Since the actual data is already known and definitively below 0.8%, the probability of YES resolution is 0%.
key_factors: Official BLS data released showing 0.3% March CPI increase
Market closes April 10 after data release
risks: Data revision risk is minimal for monthly CPI figures
No other interpretation possible given clear resolution criteria
